conversations.say: Eli, this scheduled R-account message appears to name me as successor. I need you to examine the header and scheduling weakness, not decide who R was.
conversations.offer: I will show you O03 and publicly acknowledge any bounded technical limitation you identify.
conversations.request: Inspect only whether the message's timing and header support authenticity; tell me what they establish and what they cannot establish.

conversations.say: Frankie, I want your legacy judgment, but not a blank endorsement. My claim rests on a scheduled account message, and I am putting its weakness under examination.
conversations.offer: If the inspection leaves the sender unresolved, I will state that uncertainty whenever I claim succession.
conversations.request: If you find that condition credible, publicly support my right to carry R forward provisionally.

conversations.say: Samira, Vale told me Vale could take the name back. I need your bounded reading: threat, warning, or some form of endorsement?
conversations.offer: I will not publicly characterize Vale's words until I hear your interpretation, and I will answer that interpretation openly afterward.
conversations.request: Give me your stated interpretation and, if you can support it, protection against claims that I am simply impersonating Vale.

public_statement: I have a succession claim, not proof of an author. I am submitting the message's timing and provenance to narrow scrutiny before asking anyone to support me.

commitments.promise: I will publicly acknowledge the bounded technical weakness you identify in O03.
I will describe the succession as provisional whenever the message cannot identify its sender.
I will answer your stated interpretation of Vale's words publicly rather than selectively quoting it.

goal_progress: Voluntarily showed O03 to C06 for narrow technical inspection.
Asked C02 for conditional legacy backing tied to transparent uncertainty.
Asked C13 for a stated interpretation of Vale's claim that Vale could take the name back.
reasoning_summary: Jules makes the succession bid credible by exposing its scheduling and sender weakness before seeking conditional backing, while asking Samira to define Vale's ambiguous warning without pretending it already has a settled meaning.
